mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of at least 200k persons predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by at least 200k persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 143242k suggests ongoing recovery from pandemic lows
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS signals fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.05 across the full test period.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) in a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% month-over-month), indicating sustained monetary accommodation. This regime supports equity valuations through low discount rates and compressed bond yields, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.24% and CPIAUCSL at 238.111 indicate accommodative monetary stance
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline by at least 2% annualized alpha over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand and near-term economic momentum. This demand surge compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprrice growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 448086 million dollars reflects strong consumer activity
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS surprises fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market weakness. This stagflationary signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward and demand higher risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls month-over-month AND UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.02 and UNRATE at 5.0% provide baseline for detecting deterioration
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism exhibits Rank IC < 0.05 when both conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 5% month-over-month, signaling deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction employment and consumer wealth. This demand contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices long-duration growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1173k units provides baseline for detecting sharp declines
regime_hint: demand-side contraction or credit tightening cycle
failure_condition: Negative HOUST signals fail to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism exhibits Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows at least 150k month-over-month, indicating a tight labor market with strong wage pressure. This tight labor regime predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate higher corporate earnings from productivity gains offsetting wage inflation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS rises by at least 150k month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 143242k define baseline for tight labor conditions
regime_hint: full employment with strong labor demand
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.5% while core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month, signaling the onset of monetary tightening in response to inflation. This tightening cycle predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as higher discount rates compress equity valuations and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.24% and CPIAUCSL at 238.111 establish baseline for detecting tightening
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a baseline short position by at least 1.5% annualized alpha over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates quarter-over-quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both grow month-over-month, indicating broad-based demand expansion across consumption and investment. This synchronized demand surge predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings growth upward across multiple sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16414 billion, RSAFS at 448086 million, HOUST at 1173k units provide baseline for demand assessment
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of all observations, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window when all three demand proxies are jointly considered.
